objective: Repair the governed consumer rollout procedure so it uses remote-reachable governance refs and satisfies repo-local publish gates before more product-repo propagation.
scope_boundary: Replay and classify already-merged AIS and knocktracker lanes.
No new consumer adoption lane until the governance repair is resolved.
out_of_scope: Starting another consumer rollout lane before issue #609 resolves.
Ad hoc consumer fixes without issue-backed follow-up.
Reopening governance-source specialist design from issue #589.
research_summary: Post-merge audit showed the active governance checkout was on local branch issue-385 while remote origin/main was at a different commit. Consumer lanes used governance ref da888a5bd09a40105d550658b58489cd96d3ff5e, which was present locally but not fetchable by GitHub from the remote repo. Knocktracker PR #190 also missed repo-local publish gates: file-index refresh, runner-status doc update for workflow changes, and PR-body required sections.

sprints.name: Planning packet and replay audit
sprints.goal: Capture the rollout defect, pause downstream propagation, incorporate the replay and Claude review follow-up, and define the hard-gated governance repair boundary.
tasks: Write the issue-609 PDCAR, structured plan, and planning handoff.
Replay the merged AIS and knocktracker lanes against remote governance state, including workflow-ref scope.
Document the rollout hard gates for remote refs and repo-local publish checks.
Specify the concrete remote-reachability mechanism and replay checklist.
Route the planning packet through governed alternate-family review.
acceptance_criteria: Issue #609 remains governance-only until the replay evidence and review follow-up are complete.
The packet names the remote-ref and repo-local publish-gate defects explicitly.
Governance tracker #591 records the blocked rollout state and links issue #609.
Alternate-family review is recorded before implementation promotion.
The packet defines a concrete remote-reachability mechanism and replay checklist.

specialist_reviews.focus: Classify whether the merged consumer lanes are complete or need corrective follow-up.
specialist_reviews.summary: Post-merge replay audit completed. AIS needs a corrective repin follow-up because the merged lane pinned an unreachable governance SHA, but no repo-local publish-gate miss was proven on the original PR path. Knocktracker needs a corrective consumer PR for the unreachable governance SHA plus missed repo-local publish gates: file-index refresh, runner-status doc update for workflow changes, and required PR-body sections.

alternate_model_review.reviewer: Claude specialist review via scripts/codex-review.sh claude
alternate_model_review.summary: Accepted with required follow-up: implementation must specify the remote-reachability mechanism, define a concrete replay checklist, and include workflow-ref scope in merged-lane audits.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Implement governance-source hard gates so consumer rollout fails before PR publication when governance refs are local-only or when required repo-local publish gates are missing, while downstream rollout remains paused until this repair merges.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Patch deploy/verify/runbook and rollout-checklist surfaces to enforce remote-reachable governance refs, concrete replay commands, workflow-ref scope, and repo-local publish-gate checks.
handoff_acceptance_criteria: Issue #609 remains governance-only in this branch.
The implementation defines and enforces a concrete remote-reachability mechanism.
The implementation defines and enforces a concrete replay checklist including workflow-ref scope.
The implementation enforces repo-local publish-gate checks before further consumer rollout.

material_deviation_rules: Do not start another consumer rollout lane before issue #609 resolves.
Do not treat merged consumer PRs as complete when required GitHub checks failed.
Do not pin governance refs from a local-only branch.
